intitule: Exercise 1: Reading Comprehension - Climate Change Brochure

enonce.texte:
Read the following brochure about climate change carefully:

What is Climate Change?

Climate change refers to long-term changes in temperature and weather patterns. These changes may be natural, but since the 1800s, human activities have been the main driver of climate change. The burning of fossil fuels like coal, oil, and gas produces greenhouse gases. These gases trap heat in the atmosphere, causing the Earth's temperature to rise. This is called global warming.

Effects of Climate Change:

  • Rising sea levels
  • More frequent and severe weather events (floods, droughts, hurricanes)
  • Melting of polar ice caps
  • Loss of biodiversity
  • Food and water scarcity

What can we do?

  • Reduce, reuse, and recycle
  • Use renewable energy (solar, wind)
  • Plant trees
  • Use public transport or walk
  • Save water and electricity

questions.texte: What is the main cause of climate change according to the brochure?
questions.reponse: According to the brochure, human activities, especially the burning of fossil fuels like coal, oil, and gas, are the main driver of climate change since the 1800s.

questions.texte: List three effects of climate change mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: Three effects of climate change mentioned in the text are: 1) Rising sea levels, 2) More frequent and severe weather events (floods, droughts, hurricanes), and 3) Melting of polar ice caps.

questions.texte: Explain the relationship between greenhouse gases and global warming.
questions.reponse: Greenhouse gases trap heat in the atmosphere. When we burn fossil fuels, we release more greenhouse gases, which causes more heat to be trapped. This leads to an increase in the Earth's average temperature, which is known as global warming.

intitule: Exercise 2: Direct and Indirect Speech Transformation

enonce.texte: Transform the following sentences from direct speech to indirect (reported) speech. Pay attention to tense changes, pronoun changes, and time expressions.

questions.texte: "Climate change is a serious problem," said the scientist.
questions.reponse: The scientist said that climate change was a serious problem.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain the tense change you made and why.
sousQuestions.reponse: I changed 'is' (present simple) to 'was' (past simple). This is because in reported speech, we usually shift the tense back one step when the reporting verb (said) is in the past.

questions.texte: "We will plant more trees next month," the students promised.
questions.reponse: The students promised that they would plant more trees the following month.

sousQuestions.texte: What changes did you make to the time expression and why?
sousQuestions.reponse: I changed 'next month' to 'the following month' because when we report speech, time expressions often change to reflect the perspective of the reporting moment.

questions.texte: "I have never seen such a severe flood before," said the old man.
questions.reponse: The old man said that he had never seen such a severe flood before.

intitule: Exercise 3: Complete the Dialogue on Climate Change

enonce.texte:
Complete the following dialogue between Amina and her teacher about climate change. Use the words in the box to fill in the blanks.

Words to use: temperature, fossil fuels, renewable, reduce, greenhouse, effects

questions.texte:
Amina: "Good morning, sir. Can you explain climate change to me?"
Teacher: "Of course, Amina. Climate change refers to long-term changes in ________ (1) and weather patterns."
Amina: "What causes it?"
Teacher: "The burning of ________ (2) like coal and oil produces ________ (3) gases that trap heat."
Amina: "What are the ________ (4) of climate change?"
Teacher: "Rising sea levels, floods, and droughts are some examples."
Amina: "What can we do to help?"
Teacher: "We can ________ (5) our carbon footprint by using ________ (6) energy like solar power."
questions.reponse:
1. temperature
2. fossil fuels
3. greenhouse
4. effects
5. reduce
6. renewable

intitule: Exercise 4: Creating Awareness - Write a Paragraph

enonce.texte:
Imagine you are participating in a school campaign on climate change. Write a short paragraph (100-120 words) to raise awareness about climate change and encourage your classmates to take action.

Your paragraph should:

  • Define climate change in your own words
  • Mention at least two effects
  • Suggest at least three actions people can take
  • Use at least one example of reported speech

questions.texte: Write a paragraph to raise awareness about climate change.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:

"Dear classmates, climate change is the gradual warming of our planet caused by human activities. Scientists have said that 'climate change is the greatest threat we face today.' The effects of climate change are devastating: rising sea levels threaten our coastal communities, and extreme weather events like droughts and floods destroy our crops. We must all take action now! We can plant trees to absorb carbon dioxide, reduce our use of plastic, and use public transport to decrease pollution. The government has also announced that they will invest in renewable energy. Remember, every small action counts. Let's act now to save our planet for future generations!"

intitule: Exercise 5: Analyzing a Climate Change Chart

enonce.texte:

questions.texte: Calculate the total increase in global temperature from 1880 to 2020.
Compare the temperature increase between 1880-1940 and 1980-2020. Which period had the fastest increase?
questions.reponse: From 1880 to 1940 (60 years), the temperature increased by 0.2Â°C. From 1980 to 2020 (40 years), the temperature increased by 0.8Â°C (from 0.4Â°C to 1.2Â°C). The period 1980-2020 had a much faster rate of increase (0.8Â°C in 40 years vs 0.2Â°C in 60 years).

questions.texte: Using the data, write a sentence to report what the data shows.
questions.reponse:
Example answers:

  • The data shows that global temperature has increased by 1.2Â°C since 1880.
  • According to the data, the global temperature has risen significantly, especially in the last 40 years.
  • Scientists have reported that the Earth's temperature continues to rise, with an increase of 0.8Â°C since 1980.

intitule: Exercise 6: Dialogues and Paragraphs on Climate Change

enonce.texte: Choose ONE of the following tasks to complete:

questions.texte:
Option A: Write a Dialogue

Write a dialogue (8-10 exchanges) between two friends discussing climate change. They should talk about:

  • What they have heard or read about climate change
  • The effects they have observed in their community
  • What they can do to help

Include at least two examples of reported speech in the dialogue.
questions.reponse:
Example Dialogue:

Jean: "Have you noticed how hot it is this year? I read that climate change is making our weather more extreme."
Marie: "Yes, my father said that 'the dry season is getting longer every year.' The rivers are drying up too."
Jean: "I heard on the news that many countries are suffering from floods and droughts. The scientists reported that 'we must act now to prevent worse effects.'"
Marie: "We should do something. I think we should plant more trees at school. The teacher told us that 'trees absorb carbon dioxide.'"
Jean: "That's a good idea. We can also reduce plastic waste and walk to school instead of using cars."
Marie: "My brother said that 'solar energy is the future.' Maybe our school should install solar panels."
Jean: "Let's talk to our classmates and start a climate club at school."
Marie: "Great idea! Together, we can make a difference."

questions.texte:
Option B: Complete a Paragraph

Complete the following paragraph about climate change by filling in the blanks with the words provided.

Words to use: atmosphere, carbon dioxide, deforestation, renewable, sea levels, weather, greenhouse, floods

"Climate change refers to long-term changes in ________ (1) patterns. The main cause is the increase of ________ (2) gases in the ________ (3). The burning of fossil fuels releases ________ (4), which traps heat. ________ (5) is also a major problem because trees absorb carbon dioxide. As a result, ________ (6) are rising, causing ________ (7) in coastal areas. To fight climate change, we should use ________ (8) energy sources like solar and wind power."
questions.reponse:
1. weather
2. greenhouse
3. atmosphere
4. carbon dioxide
5. Deforestation
6. sea levels
7. floods
8. renewable

intitule: Exercise 7: Report Writing on Climate Change Conference

enonce.texte:
You are a student journalist. You attended a school conference on climate change. Write a short report (150-200 words) of this event.

Your report should include:

  • What the conference was about
  • What the speakers said (use reported speech)
  • What you learned
  • Your recommendations

questions.texte: Write a report about the climate change conference.
questions.reponse:
Example Report:

Report on School Climate Change Conference

Introduction: On 15th March, our school organized a conference on climate change. The event was attended by students, teachers, and environmental experts.

Speakers: The main speaker, Dr. Mbang, explained the causes of climate change. He said that "human activities are the main drivers of global warming." He also emphasized that "we all have a responsibility to protect our environment." Another speaker, a local environmental activist, said that "deforestation is a serious problem in Cameroon." She reported that many forests are disappearing due to farming and logging.

What I learned: I learned that climate change affects everyone, especially developing countries like Cameroon. I also discovered that simple actions like planting trees and saving water can make a difference.

Recommendations: I recommend that our school start a recycling program and plant more trees around the campus. I also suggest that we organize awareness campaigns to inform more students about climate change.

Conclusion: The conference was informative and inspiring. We all must take action to fight climate change.
